Title: Why Your AI Copy Sounds Like Everyone Else
Body1_Heading: Myth: AI made good enough copy fine
Body1_P1: Everyone rushed to publish more. The feed got louder. Your posts got quieter.
Body1_P2: Good enough used to work. Now it blends in with a thousand identical posts.
Body2_P1: Two accounts can use the same prompt and sound like twins.
Body2_P2: Readers do not ignore AI. They ignore anything that feels copy-pasted.
Body3_P1: Posting daily with generic copy trains the algorithm to skip you.
Body3_P2: One post that sounds like you beats seven posts that sound like a template.
Body4_Heading: Instructions will not fix your voice
Body4_P1: Telling AI to sound witty or professional rarely works.
Body4_P2: Feed it your real writing. Examples teach voice. Adjectives do not.
Body5_P1: A slightly rough sentence in your voice outperforms a perfect paragraph in theirs.
Body5_P2: People scroll for connection. Robotic polish reads like noise.
Body6_P1: When everyone uses the same tools the tool stops being the edge.
Body6_P2: The edge is you. Your stories. Your phrasing. Your point of view.
Body7_Heading: Start with one post this week
Body7_P1: Pick a post you wrote that got real replies. Paste it into your AI workflow.
Body7_P2: Generate the next draft from that example. Compare. Notice the difference.
Takeaway: STOP CHASING VOLUME. START SOUNDING LIKE YOU.
CTA_Steps: 1) SAVE FOR LATER / 2) COMMENT VOICE FOR MY FREE GUIDE
